section.heading: Security management training for officials assigned to a high risk, high threat post

content.p: Officials described in subsection (c) who are assigned to a high risk, high threat post shall receive security training described in subsection (b) on a mandatory basis in order to improve the ability of such officials to make security-related management decisions.

subsection.heading: Security training described
subsection.chapeau: Security training referred to in subsection (a) may include—

paragraph.content: development of skills to better evaluate threats;
effective use of security resources to mitigate such threats; and
improved familiarity of available security resources.

subsection.heading: Officials described
subsection.chapeau: Officials referred to in subsection (a) are the following:

paragraph.content: Members of the Senior Foreign Service appointed under section 3942(a)(1) or 3943 of this title or members of the Senior Executive Service (as such term is described in section 3132(a)(2) of title 5).
Foreign Service officers appointed under section 3942(a)(1) of this title holding a position in classes FS–1 or FS–2.
Foreign Service Specialists appointed by the Secretary under section 3943 of this title holding a position in classes FS–1 or FS–2.
Individuals holding a position in grades GS–14 or GS–15.
Personal services contractors and other contractors serving in positions or capacities similar to the officials described in paragraphs (1) through (4).

content.p: The requirements of this section shall take effect beginning on the date that is 1 year after December 16, 2016.

section.sourceCredit: (Pub. L. 99–399, title IV, § 417, as added Pub. L. 114–323, title I, § 121(a), Dec. 16, 2016, 130 Stat. 1913.)

note.heading: References in Text
note.p: GS–14 and GS–15, referred to in subsec. (c)(4), are contained in the General Schedule, which is set out under section 5332 of Title 5, Government Organization and Employees.

note.heading: Definitions
note.p: For definition of “high risk, high threat post” as used in this section, see section 4866(d)(2) of this title.
